chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI H.C.A. No.271 of 2024 Tanveer & another Versus The Province of Sindh & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For orders on CMA 1883/24 3. For hearing of main case. 4. For orders on CMA 1884/24 Dated: 29.11.2024 Mr. Danial Shaikh for appellants. Mr. Ahmed Ali Hussain for respondent No.13. Mr. Agha Ali Khan Durrani for respondents No.16 to 22. -.-.- This appeal is arising out of an order dated 06.06.2024, which was earlier impugned in High Court Appeal No.252 of 2024. Learned counsel for the appellants submits that in fact there were two suits, which were being heard before learned Single Judge who passed the impugned order and a Division Bench in an earlier challenge observed that at the most it could be read as an interim injunction which did not require interference of the Division Bench. So are we of the view and the parties arrayed as plaintiffs and defendants in the connected suit would not make any difference since principle drawn earlier would apply mutates mutandis. Learned counsel for the appellants has already submitted that they have already moved not only a review application but also in response to the injunction application a counter-affidavit has been filed wherein details have been provided that they have share in the business, perhaps the subject matter of the litigation. Such counts shall be taken into consideration as and when the applications are being heard and decided finally b the learned Single Judge. We however deem it appropriate that it would be proper in view of suggestions of the counsels and the grounds taken that the applications pending before learned Single Judge be heard preferably in four weeks’ time.

Such counts shall be taken into consideration as and when the applications are being heard and decided finally b the learned Single Judge. We however deem it appropriate that it would be proper in view of suggestions of the counsels and the grounds taken that the applications pending before learned Single Judge be heard preferably in four weeks’ time. High Court Appeal stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed applications. Chief Justice Judge
